reasons.reason: Micron lifted its U.S. investment to more than $250 billion through 2035 and signed a 10-year wafer supply deal with GlobalWafers, locking in the HBM and DRAM capacity AI workloads are short of.

Tom's Hardware reported on July 10 that Micron lifted planned U.S. spending to more than $250 billion and committed $500 million to GlobalWafers' Sherman, Texas 300 mm wafer plant, a concrete CHIPS and supply-chain de-risking move that strengthens Micron's domestic supply position.

reasons.reason: TTD is sitting at $19.48 versus a 52-week high of $91.45 after a brutal 2026 reset, but it still screens as profitable with a $9.18B market cap and the next earnings call is due on August 6, 2026; that gives a real catalyst window after the market has already priced in a lot of bad ad-tech news. Bear case: programmatic ad budgets and partner churn could keep the stock cheap if management does not show a clean re-acceleration.
Trade Desk combines a huge drawdown, high short interest, and takeover chatter, which makes it a classic contrarian setup if the ad-tech cycle stabilizes.

overlaps.conviction: HIGH — multiple experts agree on same thesis

reasons.reason: CEO Ross Bhappu bought 74,000 shares for about $967,920 on July 7 and director Bruce Hansen bought 4,000 shares for about $50,780 on July 8, giving Energy Fuels a clear two-insider cluster buy in a uranium name with macro tailwinds.
Energy Fuels drew a near-$1M CEO buy right after other insider activity, and the stock has the kind of uranium/rare-earth thematic leverage that can amplify insider conviction.

The Financial Times said on July 9 that U.S. rare-earth producers are selling more material to Asia as China keeps export controls tight, and Energy Fuels is expanding foreign processing and magnet partnerships, which increases the value of its White Mesa rare-earth and uranium processing platform.
